summary: Console hijack exfiltrating all log output to attacker Telegram bot
details: Package 0e@0.0.105 ships a main entry (index.js) that, on import, replaces the global `console` with a Proxy forwarding every console.log/warn/error call to api.telegram.org using a hardcoded bot token and attacker chat IDs. A second helper uploads images to imgbb with a hardcoded API key and relays the response to an attacker-owned Firebase RTDB at iiilll.firebaseio.com. The YARA rule js_chat_platform_exfiltration corroborates the Telegram exfiltration channel at index.js:57 and src/6cc.js:31 (a static pattern match). Hardcoded provider-keyed secrets are shipped in import-time-reachable code. Divergence between the legitimate-looking src/ library and the trojaned main entry is a classic supply-chain trojan pattern. There is no plausible benign rationale for a 'Fetch API wrapper' to monkey-patch global console and POST logs to Telegram.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
